mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP (GDPC1) signals stronger aggregate demand, which increases corporate profits and inflation expectations, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the following month.
failure_condition: If over any 2-year rolling window the correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 20-day yield changes is less than 0.1, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.5%, investors seek safe-haven assets, driving down the 10-year yield as recession fears intensify, producing negative returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses above 6.5% the 10-year yield rises in more than 60% of 5-day windows over the following 2 years, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in CPI (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicate that the central bank is proactively combating inflation, which raises term premiums and long-term yields in the near term.
failure_condition: If in periods when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise month-over-month, the 20-day yield change is negative more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: During the industrial recovery phase where industrial production (INDPRO) growth is positive but below 5% year-over-year, further increases in INDPRO signal robust manufacturing activity, leading to higher long-term yields through stronger growth expectations.
failure_condition: If within this phase, month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by 60-day yield declines in over 40% of occurrences, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Higher monthly retail sales (RSAFS) directly reflect robust consumer spending, which boosts GDP growth expectations and thereby exerts upward pressure on the 10-year yield over the following week.
failure_condition: If over any 1-year rolling window, a month-over-month rise in RSAFS is associated with a decrease in the 10-year yield more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1 million units annualized, it signals a self-sustaining housing recovery, which lifts growth expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If after HOUST crosses above 1000, the 20-day yield change is negative in more than half of the cases over a 3-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a tightening labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to raise short-term rates, lifting long-term yields.
failure_condition: If in periods when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls month-over-month, the 20-day yield change is negative more than 40% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a low federal funds rate regime (FEDFUNDS below 3%), an increase in the funds rate signals the start of policy normalization, which increases long-term yields as the market prices in higher future short rates.
failure_condition: If within the low rate regime, a rise in FEDFUNDS is followed by a 60-day yield decline in more than 30% of cases, the hypothesis is contradicted.
